componentDescription: Switch is a toggle control for binary on/off settings. Use Switch instead of Checkbox when the action takes immediate effect without requiring a form submit (e.g., enabling a feature, toggling dark mode). SwitchGroup wraps multiple Switch components that share a name, useful for related toggles.
features: SwitchGroup compound component for grouping related switches
slot prop for supplementary React node content
compositionPattern: SwitchGroup wraps multiple Switch components. The group manages shared name and disabled state. Individual Switch components inside the group can still be individually controlled. Use SwitchGroup when presenting a set of related on/off settings under a common label.

usageExamples.title: Single controlled switch
usageExamples.description: Feature toggle that takes immediate effect when changed.
usageExamples.code:
<Switch
  id="dark-mode"
  label="Dark mode"
  subtext="Switch between light and dark theme"
  size={SwitchSize.MEDIUM}
  checked={isDarkMode}
  onChange={(checked) => setIsDarkMode(checked)}
/>

usageExamples.title: SwitchGroup with multiple toggles
usageExamples.description: Group of notification preference switches under a common label.
usageExamples.code:
<SwitchGroup
  id="notifications"
  label="Notification preferences"
  name="notifications"
>
  <Switch
    id="email-notif"
    label="Email notifications"
    subtext="Receive updates via email"
    checked={emailEnabled}
    onChange={setEmailEnabled}
  />
  <Switch
    id="sms-notif"
    label="SMS notifications"
    subtext="Receive alerts via text message"
    checked={smsEnabled}
    onChange={setSmsEnabled}
  />
  <Switch
    id="push-notif"
    label="Push notifications"
    subtext="Receive in-app push notifications"
    checked={pushEnabled}
    onChange={setPushEnabled}
  />
</SwitchGroup>

usageExamples.title: Disabled switch for locked setting
usageExamples.description: Switch that is locked due to insufficient permissions.
usageExamples.code:
<Switch
  id="2fa"
  label="Two-factor authentication"
  subtext="Managed by your organization"
  checked={true}
  disabled
  size={SwitchSize.MEDIUM}
/>

checked.llmContext: Controlled checked state. Provide boolean from state. Pair with onChange to update state. Use controlled mode for switches that affect immediate application behavior.
checked.propDescription: Controlled on/off state of the switch

onChange.llmContext: Callback fired with the new boolean value when the switch is toggled. Trigger your state update and any side effects (API calls, theme changes) here.
onChange.propDescription: Called with new boolean value when switch is toggled

defaultChecked.llmContext: For uncontrolled mode — set the initial state without managing it in React state. Suitable for preferences read only on form submit.
defaultChecked.propDescription: Initial on/off state in uncontrolled mode

label.llmContext: The primary label describing what the switch controls. Use active-voice descriptions like 'Enable notifications' or 'Allow analytics'.

subtext.llmContext: Additional description below the label explaining the consequence of toggling. E.g., 'You will receive an email for every new comment'.
subtext.propDescription: Secondary descriptive text below the label

size.llmContext: Use SMALL for dense settings panels or when the switch is inside a table or list row. Use MEDIUM for standalone or prominent settings.
size.propDescription: Controls the size of the switch toggle and text

error.llmContext: Set to true to display the switch in an error state. Rarely needed for switches, but use if the toggle state conflicts with a system requirement (e.g., a required feature that must be enabled).
error.propDescription: Applies error styling to the switch
